feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Calculate the totals of measures - Sap Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

In the CDS view source of my SAC report I had to set the MAX aggregation for the measures.
But now I can't (rightly) display the totals... if I enable the display of totals (first row of table) in the table it gives me the MAX values.
How can I manage it?

I attach an example screen

Thanks

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Rejected Records with Issue - "Data Access Control violation" in a model Import Job
entry.content:
Hello Team - We have import job (to a model) scheduled as a calendar even in our SAP Analytics Cloud tenant. The import job has a few rejected records and the rejected summary has a message, "Data Access Control violation" for all the records. 

I have checked the data access control of underlying dimension (cost center), the member has a hierarchy assigned to it and that hierarchy has the user who scheduled the calendar event in READ and WRITE columns.

There are many cost centers assigned to the same hierarchy. Data imports successfully to all the cost centers except for the two new cost centers that were defined in S4 recently.

Any inputs would be greatly helpful.

Thanks in Advance,

Regrads,

Phani

entry.title: Decimal place in a calculated field in SAC
entry.content:
Hi team

I'm having a problem to set-up correctly decimal place in a calculated field ( using formula in SAC) 

I want :

0 decimal on point 1 

Percentage as scales on point 2

 

 

What I have done without success:

1) on Styling : reset to out under "Number Format"

about point 1  ; go  on calculated field and removed decimal place 

 

 

nothing .. value continue to have 2 decimal 

Same step at point 2 ( this field is calculate using formula) but the value is not showing as %

Any suggestion ? 

Best regards

 

 

 

 

What I have alredy

entry.title: Multiplication of colunm Sap Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello all!!

I'm new and need some heIp. Is there a way to multiply the values of a measure in a data action? For example:

Equip  FDS  TYPE

A        0,97  PARALEL

B        0,66 PARALEL

C      0,88  SERIE

TOTAL = 0,97 * 0,66 * 0,88

I've tried this way but it didn't work

VARIABLEMEMBER #total OF [d/Measures]

DATA([d/Measures] = #total) = 1

 

FOREACH [d/TYPE]

DATA([d/Measures] = #total) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #total  *  RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] ="FDS")

ENDFOR

entry.title: SAC Planning Layout related
entry.content:
I have a SAC Planning reporting requirement. The excel layout used by the user is as attached in the screenshot below. 

It has different sections.

The first section (MRR), has entries at the profit center group level. Profit center group is a property added to the profit center dimension.

All other sections has entries at a GL Account group level. GL Account group is a property added to the GL Account dimension.

How can I get a similar layout as below? Also, the MRR total is considered for calculations further down for calculating total revenue and profit, and others.

Any suggestions?

entry.title: Function Product from Excel - SAP ANALYTICS CLOUD
entry.content:
Is there a function in Excel that allows me to calculate the product of values ​​
=Product(a1;a2;a3).
Is there a similar function in SAC?
I need to multiply values ​​of the same measure by filtering by a group. But by default SAC adds the values ​​and I can't multiply them. Instead of adding when aggregating, I should multiply them.
Thanks.
Sabrina.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: cannot access Feed.Color for line chart
entry.content:
I want to create a line chart for comparing different versions as differently colored lines. I'd like to assign these versions to the chart via scripts using addMember(Feed.Color, version_name). This did however not work for me, and it turns out that getMembers(Feed.Color) always returns an empty list for line charts, even though the color feed is clearly being used in the builder and the chart is showing differently colored lines.

Using getMembers(Feed.ValueAxis) on the line chart does correctly return the Measures used and using getMembers(Feed.Color) on a heat map also works as expected. Is there some special way the color feed is handled for line graphs?

Thanks in advance. Kind regards,
Paul

entry.title: Implement Treasury Executive Dashboard fiori app in on-premise SAP system
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need information regarding the implementation of the "Treasury Executive dashboard" app. The OData service(/SSB/SMART_BUSINESS_RUNTIME_SRV) and UI5 application(SACINTEGS1) are already in active state in the system. However, as mentioned in the Fiori Apps Library, it is a public cloud-based app. Is there any way to integrate this app with an on-premise system?

entry.title: Issue Setting Multiple Intervals for Variable using setVariableValue via Scripted Button in SAC?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently working on a scripted button in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to dynamically set values for a variable using the setVariableValue() method. While this works perfectly for a single interval, I’m encountering issues when trying to set multiple intervals at once.

For example, I’d like to set the variable to include the following ranges:

  • 1000–1999

  • 3000–3999

  • 6000–6999

However, when I execute the following script, only the last interval (6000–6999) is applied.:


Chart_1.getDataSource().setVariableValue("HRHN_SAC", {
from: "1000",
to: "1999",
from: "3000",
to: "3999",
from: "6000",
to: "6999",
exclude: false
});

It seems that SAC is overwriting the previous values and retaining only the final one.

Am I missing something here? Is there an alternative approach to pass multiple intervals to a variable in a single script?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Best Regards,
Feras

entry.title: SAC: Story filter not working when set to single selection
entry.content:
Hello, 

We are setting up filters for the story and we have a problem with one of the dimensions.

When setting the filter if we configure it as multiple selection it works properly: 

 

But we just one the user to be able to select one, and when we configure the filter to single selection it doesn't work. 

With the other dimensions: Sociedad and Area funcional we have set the filters as single selection without any problems. Does anyone know what is happening? 

We check the dimension in the model and we don't see any difference with the other dimension.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: U: Bug in Analytics Cloud Live Model Parameter F4 Help INA for IDs InfoObjects on Calculation Views
entry.content:
Hello,

is there an  error or bug in the Analytics Cloud Live Model with a Parameter in  the F4 Help for ID or description search ?

I get an error message while entering an ID or a description in the F4 Help of a Live Model Query Parameter. 

 

In a SAC live model Story I got a with a Query parameter for company code.
Entering in the F4 Help of  the parameter an Id or a description,
I get  the error message:

 

"
The feature requested by the client using BW InA is not implemented; you may create a development request. Class: 'CL_BICS_INA_PROC_DATA_F4',
Method:'EXECUTE',
Context:'OR selection not supported, cf. class CL_RSMD_RS_SPECIAL, SET_SELECTION_SELOPT_OR'
"

Is this still :
 2931956 - "Feature is currently not supported" message when using advanced filter and cross-tab in S...

Any sample for the Extension suggsted in the error message ?
Thank You !

Martin

entry.title: Measure Input Control Error
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have been using the measure/dimension input controls for a while now without any problems, but suddenly it shows this error when expanding the input control

Is this an update in the tool or am I missing something?

Thank you.

entry.title: Embed a SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story into a SharePoint
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We're trying to embed a SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story into a SharePoint site using the Embed web part. Both SharePoint and SAC use SAML-based Single Sign-On (SSO) via Azure AD, and user authentication works as expected.

Here's the embed code we're using:

<iframesrc="https: //XXXXXXXX.euXX.hcs.cloud.sap/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/XXXXXXX/bo/embed/story/XXXXXXXXXX" width="100%" height="600"></iframe>

What we've already done:

  • In SharePoint:
    Under Site Settings > HTML Field Security, we've added the domain:
    XXXXXXXX.euXX.hcs.cloud.sap
  • In Azure AD:
    SAP Analytics Cloud has been added as an enterprise application.
  • In SAC (under System > Administration > App Integration):
    We've added the following trusted domains:
    • https: //XXXXXXX.sharepoint.com
    • https://*.sharepoint.com

      Problem:

      When we try to view the embed web part in SharePoint, we get an error inside the iFrame. We’ve attached a screenshot of the error below for reference.

      Question:

      Has anyone successfully embedded a SAC story in SharePoint this way?

      Are there any additional configurations needed in SAC (e.g. headers, CSP, embedding permissions) or SharePoint (web part limitations, CSP settings, etc.)?

      Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

      Thanks in advance

entry.title: Import Job taking significant time to import single record via Odata services?
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created an Odata connection to import data from Datasphere to SAC. There is only single record in the view. When I'm trying to import the data via this Odata connection into my SAC model. The job is taking around 50 -60 seconds to refresh the data. There are no transformation logic defined in this import job. It is one to one mapping with the dimensions of SAC model. I'm wondering just to pull a single record why is this import job taking so much time. Anyone who has faced this similar kind of issue or knows the reason for this delay. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Joule in SAC and S/4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello. I want to ask, did anyone know is joule already available at SAP Analytics Cloud ? If yes is there any guide how to connect it?

 

Also for joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition , can joule show a bar chart or line chart ? Cause when i try its cannot

entry.title: Is there a walkaround to upload excel with different Date columns in Upload Job Starter in SAC?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We create an Upload Job using the excel file that contains columns [202501,202502,....,202512] like this:

When we want to use Upload Job Starter in story to upload the excel file that contains columns[202601,202602,...,202612], it fails. We understand that's because the columns are not same as the one already mapped in Upload Job. But is there any walkaround to meet our requirement? We don't want to create another Upload Job because we have lots of files need to upload so we have to create lots of jobs. We figured out a walkaround to tranposed the column like the picture below but it's also challenging for us to apply this considering the data volume.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: How to have measures in between dimensions in SAC Table widget?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on a project where I need to customize the layout of a table in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Specifically, I am required to position a measure between two dimensions in the table widget.

For instance, the default setup is as follows: Company Code, G/L Account, and Amount in TC. However, I need to rearrange it to: Company Code, Amount in TC, and G/L Account.

I understand that using a Measure-Based Dimension in a Calculated Dimension can achieve this.

 However, my challenge lies in the fact that my SAC table has numerous columns, and I'm unsure how to decide which columns should be included in the Dimension Context. If I add all the columns, it results in ‘null’ appearing in the calculated measure-based dimension.

Could you please guide me on how to efficiently determine which columns should be part of the Dimension Context, or if there's an alternative method to achieve the desired column arrangement without encountering null values?

entry.title: SAC - Measure percentage difference
entry.content: I need to create an indicator that performs calculations between the current month and the previous month. I’m attaching an image of the desired result: (VALUE currenth month - VALUE previous month) /  VALUE previous month

entry.title: Bug with Opening Task in Calendar - SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing some bugs when using the calendar for planning workflows. The problem is the following: we have implemented a workflow process where two users are owners of all the workflow,  User A and User B.

The problem is that when User A is the one opening and cancelling the workflow, it works fine, but if User B cancels and reopens the workflow, then it doesn't work. We don't know what's causing this behavior to happen. Here are some evidences:

 

The workflow doesn’t work when:

  1. Diego cancels the workflow.
  2. Beatriz opens the workflow.

In this case the second step a data blocking step “data”, doesn’t work and the error that is showing is the next one:

 

This error is not possible, since the data lock step should be running automatically because Diego is the owner and the assignee for the task as well as the data locking owner inside the model dimensions.

 

And here are the permissions for the data locking driving dimensions:

 

 

 

 

The workflow works when:

  1. Diego cancels the process.
  2. Diego opens the process. 

The workflow works correctly, and it doesn’t ask for any permission.

 

Any help is welcome, king regards,

Alejandro.

entry.title: Unable to add the difference in the foreach.booked with each product in SAP SAC Data action
entry.content:
Hi All,

Here is the scenario we are facing the issues, we have to read each record by record and calculate the sub total and main total also should remain same even after re-allocation at discrete level.

Measure used is a Volume ,it cannot be a decimal.

we have some pre-populated volumes from source for PNO12 with sub -totals as 45, 9 and 27 respectively.

Now user ,will re-allocate the volumes from existing customer category to a new categories like 

10A = 30 , 20A = 30 and 71A = 21, after the allocation sub totals should remain same at CC and PNO12

like 45 at 224 H1 G3 0 D 1 19 and so on.

 

Please correct this below code and suggest if any idea and it should be dynamic.

CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY =[d/DIM_BPACCT]
CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES =ON
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] ="ZVOLUME"
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_DATASRC]="CALCULATE"
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#"
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/Cutoff_Date] TO [d/Version].[p/Enddate]
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_MKT] = BASEMEMBER([d/DIM_MKT] , %UNMarket%)
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_PNO3]= BASEMEMBER([d/DIM_PNO3] ,%PNO3% )
MEMBERSET [d/DIM_VERSION]=%PlanningVersion%
//MEMBERSET [d/DIM_PNO12] =("224 H1 G3 0 D 1 19", "224 H4 G3 0 D 1 19")
VARIABLEMEMBER #Total_Amount OF  [d/Measures]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #STORE6520_COPY OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Total6520_INPUT OF [d/Measures]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #CCPercentage OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Store_6520INPUT OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Total_6520 OF [d/DIM_BPACCT]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #Subtotal_of_Each_CC OF [d/DIM_PNO12]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Difference_in_CC OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #PNO12SUBTOTAL_POSTALLOCATION OF  [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
 
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST1 OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST2 OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST3 OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #TEST4 OF [d/Measures]
 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Store OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #Store2 OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #TOTALOFPREVIOUS OF [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Account OF [d/DIM_BPACCT]  
VARIABLEMEMBER #CC OF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
VARIABLEMEMBER #SubTotatofPNO12 OF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
VARIABLEMEMBER #CCtotal OF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #FINDDIFF OF  [d/Measures]
VARIABLEMEMBER #VOLSTORE OF [d/Measures] 
VARIABLEMEMBER #FINALSTORE OF [d/Measures] 
INTEGER @count
INTEGER @CurrentCount
INTEGER @GETTOTAL
 
//Simple copy from 6520_COPY to store it in Variable member
DATA( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#STORE6520_COPY ) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]="ZVOLUME")
 
//Store User Input values 6520_INPUT Example: 10A-27 and 20A-27
DATA([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT" ) =RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT")
 
//Subtotal of Each PNO12 Example: 45 , 9 (Account: 6520_COPY)
DATA( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12 ) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]="ZVOLUME")
 
 
//Store user inputted values example, 27+27 = 54 will get here (Account: 6520_INPUT)
DATA([d/Measures]=#Total6520_INPUT, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CCtotal,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" )
         = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#")
 
 
//Find the Percentage for user Inputted values Example: 27/54, 27/54 (Account: 6520_INPUT)  
DATA([d/Measures]=#CCPercentage,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" ) =( RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#")/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Total6520_INPUT, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CCtotal,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" ))
 
//Multiply Percentage with PNO12 Subtotal Example: 0.5*45, 0.5*45  AND 0.5*9 , 0.5*9  (Account: 6520_COPY)//
//Volume will be 22, 22 AND 4, 4 due to integer measure
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#CCPercentage,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" )*RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)
 
//Store the same calculation into variable member to get the decimal values (Original allocated values,Example: 22.5, 22.5 AND 4.5, 4.5)
DATA([d/Measures]=#VOLSTORE, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#CCPercentage,[d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_ENGINE]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_INDICATOR]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_PRD_ID]="#", [d/DIM_SV]="#" )*RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)
 
 
//PNO12 Subtotal after allocation Example: After allocation 22+22-->44 and  4+4-->8 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#PNO12SUBTOTAL_POSTALLOCATION) =RESULTLOOKUP([d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]="ZVOLUME",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")
 
 
//Each CC subtotal after allocation, Example : 10A=22+4->26 , 20A=22+4-->26
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_PNO12]=#Subtotal_of_Each_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")
 
//Find difference between Pre Allocated and Post Allocated... 10A(PreAlloc) - 10A(PostAlloc)
DATA([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT",[d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#")- RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_PNO12]=#Subtotal_of_Each_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")
 
 
 
 
//1st PNO12
IF [d/DIM_PNO12]!="#" THEN
 
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_PNO12]
 
@count=@Count+1
IF @count=1 THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#PNO12SUBTOTAL_POSTALLOCATION) THEN
IF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=%CustomerCategory% THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_PNO12]=#Subtotal_of_Each_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT",[d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_FACTORY]="#")  THEN
 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#" )
 
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDFOR
ENDIF
 
 
 
//2nd PNO12
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST1, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST2, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC)= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
 
IF [d/DIM_PNO12]!="#" THEN
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_PNO12]
@count=@Count+1
//FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
IF @count=2 THEN
IF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=%CustomerCategory% THEN
IF RESULTLOOKUP( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST2, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC) THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST1, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID]) THEN
 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#" )
 
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
//ENDFOR
ENDFOR
ENDIF
 
 
//3rd PNO12
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST3, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
DATA([d/Measures]=#TEST4, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC)= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
 
 
IF [d/DIM_PNO12]!="#" THEN
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_PNO12]
@count=@Count+1
//FOREACH.BOOKED [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]
IF @count=3 THEN
IF [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=%CustomerCategory% THEN
IF RESULTLOOKUP( [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/Measures]=#Total_Amount, [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#SubTotatofPNO12)!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST4, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=#CC) THEN
 
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#TEST3, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID])
!= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Store_6520INPUT, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_INPUT", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_PNO12]="#",[d/DIM_CUSTCAT]=[d/DIM_CUSTCAT].[p/ID]) THEN
 
DATA([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="ZVOLUME", [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#")+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]=#Difference_in_CC, [d/DIM_BPACCT]="6520_COPY" , [d/DIM_PNO12]="#", [d/DIM_MODELYR]="#", [d/DIM_FACTORY]="#" )
 
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDIF
ENDFOR
//ENDFOR
ENDIF
